task: iter_slices in utils.py uses a manual position counter and while loop. Replace it with a range-based loop.
correctness_argument: `range(0, len(string), slice_length)` steps through every starting offset including the final partial chunk. The slice expression `string[pos:pos+slice_length]` naturally truncates at the string boundary, so no tail is ever dropped and a string of exactly one slice_length yields one element.
